text: - b. Employee. Every person who performs the work of a laborer or mechanic is 'employed' regardless of any contractual relationship which may be alleged to exist between a contractor or subcontractor and such person.  This means that even if there is a contract between a contractor and a worker, the contractor must make sure that the worker is paid at least as much as the wage rate on the wage decision for the classification of work they perform. Note that there are no exceptions to the prevailing wage requirements for relatives or for self-employed laborers and mechanics.
